data.title: Exel Composites' Shareholders' Nomination Board's proposal to the Annual General Meeting 2025
data.description:
Exel Composites

EXEL COMPOSITES PLC        STOCK EXCHANGE RELEASE       10 JANUARY 2025 at 10:00 EET

Exel Composites Plc’s Shareholders’ Nomination Board presents the following proposals to the Annual General Meeting 2025:

Number of members and composition of the Board of Directors

The Nomination Board proposes that the number of members of the Board of Directors shall be confirmed to be five (5) and that the current members of the Board of Directors Jouni Heinonen, Helena Nordman-Knutson, and Kirsi Sormunen be re-elected as members of the Board of Directors and Christian Busdiecker and Elisabeth Larsson be elected as new members of the Board of Directors for the term ending at the closure of the Annual General Meeting of 2026. Of the current members, Petri Helsky and Jouko Peussa have informed that they are not available for re-election to the Board of Directors for the new term.

The Nomination Board further proposes Jouni Heinonen to be re-elected as Chairman of the Board of Directors for the term ending at the closure of the Annual General Meeting of 2026.

All persons proposed for Board service have given their consent to serving on the Board and are considered to be independent of the Company and its shareholders.

Proposed new members

Christian Busdiecker (born 1979, German citizen) is Managing Partner at Greentech Partners in Germany. He has previously held several leadership positions in service business, technology management and business development at Nordex, among others. He holds a Master’s degree in Business Administration and Mechanical Engineering.

Elisabeth Larsson (born 1970, Swedish citizen) is Senior Vice President, Sales and Services at Volvo Energy in Sweden, and has previously held several other leadership positions at Volvo Group in Europe and Asia. She holds a Master’s degree in Business Administration and Economics.

The CV information of the proposed new Board members is attached to this release. The proposed current members of the Board of Directors are presented on Exel Composites’ website at www.exelcomposites.com.

Remuneration of the Board of Directors

The Nomination Board proposes that the annual remuneration for the Board members remain unchanged as follows:

  • Chairman: Annual remuneration of EUR 45,000 (previous year: EUR 45,000) and additionally EUR 1,500 (1,500) for attendance at each Board and committee meeting and for each other similar all-day Board assignment.
  • Board member: Annual remuneration of EUR 21,000 (21,000) and additionally EUR 1,000 (1,000) for attendance at each Board and committee meeting and each other similar all-day Board assignments. In addition, for each committee meeting, the meeting fee for the committee chairman shall beEUR 1,500 (1,500).
  • Travel expenses and other out-of-pocket expenses arising from the Board work will be compensated in accordance with the Company’s established practice and travel rules.

Out of the yearly remuneration 60% would be paid in cash and 40% in Company’s shares.

The Annual General Meeting is planned to be held on 26 March 2025. The proposals will also be included in the Notice to the Annual General Meeting 2025 to be published at the later date. 

Composition of the Shareholders’ Nomination Board

The composition of the Nomination Board preparing the proposal for the Annual General Meeting 2025:

  • Markus Lindqvist, Aktia, Chairman of the Shareholders' Nomination Board
  • Matias Arola, Danske Invest Finnish Equity Fund
  • Jukka Vähäpesola, Elo Mutual Pension Insurance Company
  • Petteri Vaarnanen, Sp-Fund Management Company and
  • Jouni Heinonen, Chairman of the Board of Directors, Exel Composites Plc, as an expert member of the Shareholders' Nomination Board

For further information, please contact:

Lilli Riikonen, Head of Investor Relations

investor@exelcomposites.com

tel. +358 50 351 1128

Exel Composites in brief

Exel Composites is one of the largest manufacturers of composite profiles and tubes made with pultrusion and pullwinding technologies and a pultrusion technology forerunner in the global composite market. Our forward-thinking composite solutions made with continuous manufacturing technologies serve customers in a wide range of industries around the world. You can find our products used in applications in diverse industrial sectors such as wind power, transportation and building and infrastructure.

Our R&D expertise, collaborative approach and global footprint set us apart from our competition. Our composite solutions help customers save resources, reduce products' weight, improve performance and energy efficiency, and decrease total lifetime costs. We want to be the first choice for sustainable composite solutions globally.

Headquartered in Finland, Exel Composites employs over 600 forward-thinking professionals around the world and is listed on Nasdaq Helsinki. To find out more about our offering and company please visit www.exelcomposites.com.

 

Exel Composites Plc

data.title: Coor extends Nordic IFM agreement with PostNord
data.description:
Coor

Coor extends Nordic IFM agreement with PostNord. The assignment will have an annual contract value of some SEK 155 M. The contract is for a period of two years and includes an option to extend it for a further two years. 

The new assignment starts on 1 July 2025. The delivery includes cleaning, outdoor environment, internal waste management, coffee & water dispensers, as well as janitorial and internal services to PostNord’s properties in the Nordics, with a total building area of some 2,000,000 m2.

“We’re delighted that PostNord has shown us this confidence. We look forward to continuing to develop service delivery to PostNord with a focus on flexibility, quality, and innovation. The agreement is further confirmation of our position as Nordic market leader in IFM”, commented Peter Viinapuu, acting President and CEO at Coor.

For more information please contact:

Peter Viinapuu, acting President and CEO, Coor

+46 766 411 006

Peter.viinapuu@coor.com

 

Andreas Engdahl, CFO and IR Director, Coor

+46 10 559 54 63

andreas.engdahl@coor.com

Magdalena Öhrn, Communications Director, Coor

+46 10 559 55 19

magdalena.ohrn@coor.com

About Coor:

As the leading provider of facility management services, Coor aims to create the happiest, healthiest, and most prosperous workplace environments in the Nordic region. Coor offers specialist expertise in workplace services, property services and strategic advisory services. Coor creates value by executing, developing, and streamlining our customers’ service activities. This enables our customers to do what they do best.

Coor’s customer base includes many large and small companies and public-sector organisations across the Nordic region, including ABB, Aibel, Alleima, the Danish Building and Property Agency, DNV, DSB, Equinor, ICA, IKEA, Karolinska University Hospital in Solna, PKA - “Danish Police, Public Prosecution Authority and Prison and Probation Service”, PostNord, Saab, SAS, Skanska, Swedbank, Telia Company, Vasakronan and Volvo Cars.

Coor was founded in 1998 and has been listed on Nasdaq Stockholm since 2015. Coor takes responsibility for the operations it conducts, in relation to its customers, employees and shareholders, as well as for its wider impact on society and the environment. Read more at www.coor.com

data.title: Prada among suitors looking at Versace, newspaper reports
data.description:
Italy's Prada , is among potential suitors examining the acquisition of fashion group Versace which Capri Holdings has put up for sale, daily Il Sole 24 ore reported on Friday.

In November Coach-owner Tapestry dropped a $8.5 billion deal to buy Michael Kors-owner Capri .

After that deal to create a U.S. luxury conglomerate fell through, Capri hired Barclays to look at strategic options including the sale of its Versace and Jimmy Choo brands, two people with direct of the knowledge of the matter told Reuters.

One person did not rule out the entire Capri Holdings group could also be up for sale.

Prada is looking at the file with Citi, the report said. Citi has worked with Prada in the past over a dual-listing project which was put on hold.

Contacted by Reuters, Prada declined to comment.

In November, Capri posted a bigger-than-expected drop in quarterly revenue, hurt by execution missteps and a global slowdown in luxury goods demand.

data.title: Berenberg expects macro to set luxury tone in 2025, more M&A
data.description:
** In a note on 2025 outlook for luxury, Berenberg says the economic environment will dominate the sector's performance and expects M&A to pick up as growth discontinues

** "We believe the market overestimates the impact of US tariffs, ignoring the offset from a stronger dollar, but underestimates the secular headwinds facing China" - Berenberg

** It sees the secular headwinds of demographics, debt and politics leading to disappointment in the "all-important" China luxury spend

** The broker also notes that while it is upbeat on the American consumer in the medium- to longer-term, it is too early for that to offset weak Chinese demand

** It sees tariffs by the U.S. President-elect Donald Trump as "two sides of the same coin", with dollar strength likely muting the negative tariffs impact

** In such sector set-up, it opts for defensive stock strategy, favouring the market share winners Brunello Cucinelli , Hermes and LVMH

** It adds that cash-rich players, such as LVMH and Richemont , will be in a position to allocate capital into higher growth luxury segments and/or buy fallen angels

data.title: UK debt office announces syndicate details for sale of 4.375% 2040 gilt
data.description:
Britain's government debt office said on Friday it had appointed banks to lead a syndicated sale of its 4.375% 2040 gilt in the week commencing Jan. 20.

The joint bookrunners were Deutsche Bank, JPMorgan, Morgan Stanley, Nomura and RBC CM.

data.title: Romania Trade Deficit Widens
data.description:
Romania's trade deficit rose to EUR 2.84 billion in November 2024 from EUR 2.32 billion in the corresponding month of the previous year, as imports rose much faster than exports.

Imports climbed 6.2% to EUR 11.23 billion, as purchases from non-EU countries increased significantly (19.8%), while imports from the EU also rose (1.8%).

Meanwhile, exports grew 1.6% to EUR 8.39 billion, amid higher sales to non-EU countries (4.3%) and the EU (0.6%).

For the first eleven months of the year, the country’s trade gap widened to EUR 30.05 billion from EUR 25.54 billion in the same month last year.

data.title: Chinese Shares Fall Amid Fears of Rising Geopolitical Tensions; Suzhou Sepax Technologies Surges 379% in Shanghai Debut
data.description:
Chinese shares ended the week with big losses as investors avoided riskier assets on expectations that geopolitical tensions between China and the US will worsen under US President-elect Donald Trump.

The Shanghai Composite Index, the main gauge of Chinese stocks, was down 1.3% to 3,168.52. The Shenzhen Component Index fell 1.8% to 9,795.94.

The blacklisting of Tencent Holdings and CATL earlier in the week further fanned fears of worsening tensions ahead of Trump's inauguration. Pessimism about China's economy amid a housing crisis and deflationary pressures also weighed down on market sentiment.

"It reflects the numerous uncertainties going on with weakening macro numbers, Trump inauguration, currency pressure due to US dollar strength, and a lull in stimulus until Two-Sessions," Bloomberg news cited Xin-Yao Ng, an investment director at abrdn Plc, as saying.

In company news, Juneway Electronic Technology closed 247% higher versus its initial public offering price of 10.40 yuan per share in its debut on the Shenzhen bourse. Suzhou Sepax Technologies surged 379% from its IPO price of 4.32 yuan per share in its debut on the Shanghai bourse.

data.title: Wheaton Precious Metals CFO to Step Down; Successor Named
data.description:
Wheaton Precious Metals on Friday said Chief Financial Officer Gary Brown will resign, with effect from March 31.

Consequently, the precious metals streaming company named Vice President of Finance Vincent Lau as Brown's successor.

data.title: French Monthly Household Consumption Up 0.3% in November 2024
data.description:
France's monthly household consumption grew 0.3% in November 2024, following a revised 0.3% decline a month ago, according to data from statistics agency Insee published Friday.

Analysts expected a 0.2% increase for the month.

data.title: Tata Capital Housing Finance to issue 5-year bonds, bankers say
data.description:
India's Tata Capital Housing Finance plans to raise 15 billion rupees ($174.7 million), including a greenshoe option of 12 billion rupees, selling bonds maturing in five years, three bankers said on Friday.

The housing finance company has invited bids from bankers and investors for the issue on Monday, they said.

The company did not immediately reply to a Reuters request for comment.

data.title: Court Orders Yonyu Plastics Chairman to Return Seals in Y.Y. Cable Lawsuit
data.description:
Yonyu Plastics received a civil judgment from the Tainan District Court involving a lawsuit filed by Y.Y. Cable Accessories.

The court ordered the company chairman to return seals numbered 11 and 12, featuring the Wei-Cheng Wang design, to Y.Y. Cable, according to a Thursday Taiwan Exchange filing.

Yonyu Plastics has engaged a lawyer to handle the legal procedures and is considering whether to appeal, it noted.

data.title: Market Chatter: Singapore Premium Grocery Segment's Outlook Strengthens on Robust Spending Data, DBS Says
data.description:
DBS Group Research said consumers' growing preference for at-home meals bodes well for the long-term growth outlook of Singapore's premium grocery market, The Edge Singapore reported Wednesday.

The premium grocery sector has pointed to a rebound post-pandemic, supported by data showing higher spending per person among private households, the report cited DBS analysts as saying.

On the other hand, the food and beverage retail segment has weakened following the pandemic, the report cited the analysts as saying.

Dampened private consumption in China, a boost in foreign funding for Southeast Asian economies, and margin improvements due to normalizing commodity prices define the sector's near-term prospects, the report cited the equity research firm as saying.

DBS selected DFI Retail Group Holdings and Thai Beverage as its top picks in the sector, the report said.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Nordex to Deliver Turbines to Four Spanish Wind Farms
data.description:
Nordex secured orders to deliver wind turbines to four unnamed customers for wind farm projects totaling 259 megawatts in Spain.

The German wind power company will supply 27 N163/5.X, 13 N163/6.X, and two N149/5.X wind turbines, with service agreements of between 10 and 25 years, according to a Friday release.

Installation will commence in autumn and commissioning is expected to start by the end of 2025.

data.title: Singapore Central Bank Could Hold Fire Until Trump Policy Becomes More Clear — Market Talk
data.description: The Monetary Authority of Singapore is in no rush to ease monetary policy settings, CIMB analysts say, reiterating their call for a hold until April. Although inflation appears to be easing, the central bank will likely err on the side of caution, they write in a note. MAS may want to wait to assess the impact of U.S. President-elect Trump's policies first, they say. Significant trade, growth and inflation uncertainty stemming from Trump's policies could lead to some market volatility after his inauguration later this month, they say. Other events to look out for include Singapore's budget in February and geopolitical risks in Eastern Europe, the analysts add.(amanda.lee@wsj.com)

data.title: Clarkson Expects Results Ahead of Market Views
data.description:
By Anthony O. Goriainoff

Clarkson said it expects full-year results for 2024 to be slightly ahead of current market expectations.

The London-listed provider of shipping services said Friday that underlying pretax profit--which strips out exceptional and other one-off items--is expected to be no less than 115 million pounds ($141.5 million) for 2024, subject to audit. Company-compiled market expectations for adjusted pretax profit are 111.3 million pounds.

For 2023, the company reported underlying pretax profit of 109.2 million pounds.

Write to Anthony O. Goriainoff at anthony.orunagoriainoff@dowjones.com

data.title: Vodafone Sells Remaining 3% Share in Indus Towers; Increases Stake in India's Vodafone Idea
data.description:
By Najat Kantouar

Vodafone Group sold its remaining 3% stake in telecom tower company Indus Towers and used the 28.0 billion Indian Rupees ($325.9 million) raised in proceeds to repay debt and increase its stake in India's Vodafone Idea.

The U.K. telecommunication group said Friday that it has completed the placing of its 79.2 million shares in Indus Towers via an accelerated bookbuild offering, as previously announced in December.

Part of the proceeds, representing 8.9 billion Indian Rupees, have been used to fully repay its existing debt secured against Vodafone's Indian assets and settle transaction fees.

Additionally, 19.1 billion Indian Rupees have been used to acquire 1.7 billion equity shares in Vodafone idea, increasing Vodafone's stake to 24.39% from 22.56%.

Vodafone has been gradually reducing its stake in Indus Towers over time. In June, the group sold 484.7 million shares through an accelerated placing to pay off its existing debt of 1.8 billion euros ($1.89 billion) secured against Vodafone's Indian assets.

Write to Najat Kantouar at najat.kantouar@wsj.com

data.title: Bang & Olufsen Confirms Guidance as It Prepares to Accelerate Strategic Plan — Update
data.description:
By Dominic Chopping

Bang & Olufsen maintained its full-year guidance and said it is now ready to accelerate its strategic plan to drive profitable growth.

The Danish consumer-electronics company has a strategic plan to enhance its focus on the customer experience, strengthen its positioning within the luxury segment and ensure high product quality.

The company recently raised 228 million Danish kroner ($31.5 million) through a share issue, with funds earmarked to strengthen its position in the luxury audio market by increasing brand awareness, optimizing its retail network and investing in new products.

It said Friday that to ensure better and faster execution of its growth plans it is changing its sales organization. By mid-January it will consolidate its three regional sales functions into a single global sales function, while establishing dedicated functions for new partnerships and adding more local sales and marketing resources.

These changes will enhance its focus on key cities and allow it to better serve customers, it said.

The company posted a net loss of 13 million kroner in its fiscal second quarter ended Nov. 30 as revenue fell 0.3% to 698 million kroner.

Analysts had expected a net profit of 4 million kroner on revenue of 687 million kroner, according to a FactSet poll.

Chief Executive Kristian Tear said this fiscal year is transitional for the company, and the second quarter was in line with its expectations and plans.

"The EMEA region and the Americas generated positive revenue growth year-on-year, while group revenue declined marginally as the Chinese market continues to be challenged," he said.

The gross margin rose to 53.7% in the quarter from 53.1% in the same period a year prior.

For the fiscal year ahead, it still expects revenue growth in local currencies of between minus 3% to positive 3%, with an earnings before interest and taxes margin before special items of minus 2% to positive 1%. Free cash flow is expected between zero and minus 100 million kroner.

In the medium term, Bang & Olufsen still targets organic revenue growth of 8% in the three-year period to fiscal 2028, with an EBIT margin before special items of 8% and free cash flow of 250 million kroner in fiscal 2028.

Write to Dominic Chopping at dominic.chopping@wsj.com

data.title: GuoCheng Mining to Offload Stake in Mining Subsidiary for 1.60 Billion Yuan
data.description:
GuoCheng Mining plans to sell 65% of subsidiary Chifeng Yubang Mining for 1.60 billion yuan, according to a Shenzhen Stock Exchange disclosure on Thursday.

Xingye Silver & Tin Mining will buy the stake.

After the completion of the transaction, the GuoCheng Mining will no longer own any equity in Yubang Mining.

GuoCheng Mining's shares closed less than 3% lower.

data.title: Airbus Deliveries Slip, But 2024 Total Supported by December Push — Market Talk
data.description: Airbus's plane deliveries for 2024 came 4% short of the company's original guidance, but the 766 deliveries it made are essentially in line with consensus estimates and the company's own downgraded target, Berenberg analysts say. The European plane maker delivered 123 planes in December, which is 10%, or 11 planes, more compared with December 2023, the analysts say in a research note. The total for last month, while impressive, "is likely to lead to a very slow start to 2025 and the increasing Q4 weighting is becoming progressively harder to get comfortable with," the analysts say. Such a push in the last month of the year has historically been followed by light deliveries in the first quarter of the following year, the analysts say. (pierre.bertrand@wsj.com)
